idea-plugin.name: PHP LSP
idea-plugin.description:
Brings the full power of php-lsp — a blazing-fast PHP language server written in Rust — directly into PhpStorm.

Built on Rust's zero-cost abstractions and memory safety, php-lsp delivers near-instant responses for completions, diagnostics, hover info, and go-to-definition — even in large codebases where traditional PHP tooling slows down.

Features:
  • Completions with minimal latency
  • Real-time diagnostics powered by a native Rust engine
  • Go-to-definition and hover documentation
  • Low memory footprint compared to JVM-based language servers
Requires the LSP4IJ plugin (free, by Red Hat). The php-lsp binary is bundled — no separate installation needed.

Source code: github.com/jorgsowa/php-lsp-phpstorm-plugin
idea-plugin.change-notes:
0.1.4
  • Update bundled php-lsp binaries to v0.1.54
0.1.3
  • Fix workspace/inlineValue/refresh UnsupportedOperationException crash
  • Update bundled php-lsp binaries to v0.1.52
0.1.2
  • Fix compatibility with PhpStorm 2025.1 and later versions
0.1.1
  • Updated description and source code link
  • Added vendor URL
0.1.0
  • Initial release
  • Bundled php-lsp v0.1.51 binaries for macOS, Linux, and Windows
  • Automatic platform detection and binary extraction at runtime

server.name: PHP LSP
server.description: PHP language server written in Rust.
